title: Expeditions Led by Muhammad
description: Then the Messenger of God led an expedition in Rabi' al-Akhir (which began October 2, 623) in search of Quraysh. He went as far as Buwat in the region of Radwa and then returned without any fighting." He stayed in Medina for the rest of Rabi' al-Akhir and part of Jumada al-01A (which began October 31, 623) and then led another expedition in search of Quraysh.29 He took the mountain track of the Banii Dinar b. al-Najjar (of Medina) and then crossed the desert of al-Khabar, halting beneath a tree in the Batha' Ibn Azhar called Dhat al-Saq.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 07
